channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Strikes Hit Infrastructure Sites in Iran After Trump’s Threat
item.description: President Trump celebrated an attack on a major highway bridge outside of Tehran after vowing to take Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” And a leading public health institution in the country was destroyed.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times

item.title: In a Muzzled Russia, He Still Speaks His Mind
item.description: While hundreds of other journalists fled into exile after Moscow’s invasion of Ukraine in 2022, the Nobel Peace Prize laureate Dmitri A. Muratov stayed. But he did not stay quiet.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dmitri A. Muratov at his office at Novaya Gazeta in Moscow in December.

item.title: After Sting Operation, Cousin of Bashar al-Assad Convicted in Arms for Drugs Deal
item.description: The cousin, Antoine Kassis, was found guilty of conspiracy to support a terrorist group, after trying to sell weapons from the fallen regime to a Colombian militia.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: A defaced billboard of former Syrian leader Bashar al Assad, in Aleppo, last year.

item.title: China’s Aiming for the Moon, and NASA Is Looking Over Its Shoulder
item.description: The U.S. space agency launched a lunar flyby Wednesday, but Beijing is pursuing its own space program with formidable focus. Here’s what we know about it, in photos and videos.
item.dc:creator: Selam Gebrekidan and Joy Dong

item.title: Russia Is Sending a Second Oil Tanker to Fuel-Starved Cuba
item.description: The announcement of a second shipment follows the arrival of a first oil tanker sent by the Kremlin earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola
item.media:description: The Russian-flagged oil tanker Anatoly Kolodkin arriving in Matanzas Bay, Cuba, on Tuesday.

item.title: Pope Leo Returns to Tradition, Washing Priests’ Feet
item.description: Pope Francis washed and kissed the feet of prisoners and refugees. On Thursday, his successor performed the rite of humility and service for priests.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV washed and kissed the feet of Roman priests on Thursday at the Basilica of St. John Lateran in Rome.

item.title: U.K. to Host Talks on Securing Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It was not clear whether the talks, expected to involve dozens of countries, would satisfy President Trump’s demand that other nations take a more active part in the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper of Britain, center, hosted a virtual meeting of foreign ministers on Thursday to discuss fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Try Living in Cape Town, Where 70% of Downtown Housing Is for Tourists
item.description: A shortage of affordable housing in the coastal city in South Africa has forced many people to live far outside the city center, while tourists occupy prime real estate.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Joao Silva

item.title: Europe Pushes for a Gentler Internet for Children
item.description: The European Union and national capitals are trying to make social media and algorithms less addictive and safer, especially for children.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.media:credit: Andrea Mantovani for The New York Times
item.media:description: Stéphanie Mistre, whose daughter, Marie Le Tiec, ended her life in 2021 at age 15, has become an activist for children’s online safety.

item.title: Evacuated From War-Torn Gaza, 11 Toddlers Return Home to Hugs and Tears
item.description: The Palestinian children were born prematurely in the early days of the war and moved from a besieged hospital to Egypt. They have now returned home to the enclave, which lies in ruins.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Bilal Shbair and Iyad Abuheweila
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sondos al-Kurd with her daughter, Bisan, 2, after they were reunited in Gaza on Tuesday.

item.title: U.S. Plans Military Expansion in Greenland
item.description: The Pentagon is in talks with Denmark for access to three more areas on the Arctic island. Several Greenlanders said they didn’t like the idea.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman, Maya Tekeli and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance and his wife, Usha, meeting with members of the U.S. military at Pituffik Space Base in Greenland last year. The missile defense installation is the only active American base on the island.

item.title: Lost Friendships, Broken Relationships: How the War in Iran Is Dividing the Diaspora
item.description: Amid months of protest, repression and war in their native country, Iranians living abroad are navigating their biggest rifts yet.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ilvy Njiokiktjien for The New York Times
item.media:description: Osveh Varastegan, who left Iran in 2020, at the gym where she works as a personal trainer in Rotterdam, the Netherlands.

item.title: Every Trump Threat to Abandon NATO Hollows It Out
item.description: Doubts that the United States would come to the aid of NATO allies increase each time, prompting Europeans to consider an alliance without Washington.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: U.S. soldiers in a military exercise with NATO members in Romania in June. The U.S. secretary of state has warned that relations with NATO will need to be re-examined.

item.title: When Racism Is a Crime: Brazil Puts a Tourist on Trial for Word and Gesture
item.description: An Argentine woman, who is white, could face years in prison after being accused of racism. The case has set off intense debate in Argentina and Brazil.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova and Lucía Cholakian Herrera
item.media:description: Agostina Páez arriving at the Argentine Consulate in Rio de Janeiro this month to attend a news conference.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman and Johnatan Reiss

item.title: Myanmar Junta Chief Ascends to President, Five Years After Coup
item.description: U Min Aung Hlaing’s elevation to the civilian post is the conclusion of elections in the country, which were stage managed by the military.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock
item.media:description: Myanmar’s military ruler, Min Aung Hlaing, after casting hist vote in Naypyidaw, the capital, in December. He was elected president on Friday by the country’s rubber-stamp Parliament.

item.title: Cosmopolitan Shanghai Is an Uneasy Fit in China’s Narrative of Western Sins
item.description: Shanghai’s many layers of architecture, culture and politics have made it a difficult fit for the Communist Party’s preferred narrative of Chinese victimhood and Western sins.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Qilai Shen
item.media:description: The Shanghai History Museum, housed in the former Shanghai Race Club.

item.title: In Syria, Kidnappings of Women and Girls Fuel a Minority Group’s Fears
item.description: A Times investigation found that abductions of women and girls from Syria’s Alawite minority were more common, and more brutal, than the government has acknowledged.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Laura Boushnak
item.media:description: A 16-year-old girl who said she was kidnapped and raped, and later found out she was pregnant.

item.title: Cuba Says It Is Pardoning More Than 2,000 Prisoners
item.description: The Cuban government said the releases were a humanitarian gesture during Holy Week. It was not clear if they were related to ongoing negotiations between Cuba and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and David C. Adams
item.media:description: Havana in January. The Cuban Embassy in Washington described the latest pardons as a “humanitarian and sovereign gesture.”

item.title: Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Problem
item.description: Our White House reporter Zolan Kanno-Youngs parses some of President Trump’s conflicting messages about the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Christina Thornell, Coleman Lowndes and Whitney Shefte
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: President Trump’s prime-time address received a defiant response from a top leader in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Workers inspecting the damage from a strike by Iran on Petah Tikva, Israel, on Thursday.

item.title: Macron Voices Europe’s Frustration With Trump’s Handling of Iran War
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron of France expressed disapproval about President Trump’s handling of the war against Iran on Thursday, chastising him for speaking cavalierly in a speech.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler, Erika Solomon and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France in Seoul on Thursday. Addressing President Trump’s verbal attacks against NATO, he said, “If you create doubt every day about your commitment, you hollow it out.”

item.title: Iranians hold a nature festival in the shadow of war.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: The Race for the Future of the Moon
item.description: The Artemis II mission isn’t just about science and exploration. It’s part of a high-stakes space race between the U.S. and China.
item.dc:creator: Selam Gebrekidan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A Bid to Use Force to Open Strait of Hormuz Hits Roadblocks at U.N. Security Council
item.description: Several permanent members of the Security Council opposed the resolution, drafted by Bahrain in coordination with its Gulf neighbors, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships sailing through the Persian Gulf and toward the Strait of Hormuz last month. Traffic through the Strait has slowed drastically since the start of the war.

item.title: Trump Hails Strike on Iranian Bridge, Warning ‘More to Follow’
item.description: Iran state media said eight people had been killed in the attack and dozens wounded. A local official said the bridge, in the mountains, was not yet in operation and that the people had been nearby when the attack happened.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Edward Wong, Parin Behrooz and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump at the Oval Office on Tuesday. Mr. Trump celebrated a strike on a bridge between Tehran and Karaj, Iran, in a social media post on Thursday.

item.title: Trump’s Cuts Have Eviscerated Once-Bipartisan Foreign Aid Programs
item.description: Acting mostly with approval from the Republican-led Congress, President Trump clawed back money it had approved for initiatives that enjoyed strong backing from members of both parties.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.media:credit: Adam Dean for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rohingya refugees after crossing into Bangladesh fleeing violence in 2017.

item.title: Liamine Zeroual, Algerian President During Bloody Civil War, Dies at 84
item.description: Cutting short his five-year term amid the scarring conflict, he was perhaps a first: an Algerian leader who left without being forced out or dying in office.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.media:description: Liamine Zeroual campaigning for the Algerian presidency in 1995. Once in office, he appeared unable or unwilling to stop the spiraling violence pitting Islamist insurgents against government forces.

item.title: Iran Says It Will Oversee Strait of Hormuz Traffic, Even in Peacetime
item.description: The United States says Iran’s claims to control the strait are illegal, but Tehran has made escalating assertions of authority over the vital waterway.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A tanker anchored off the coast of Muscat, Oman, last month. At its narrowest, the Strait of Hormuz sits entirely within the territorial waters of Oman and Iran.

item.title: Trump Says He’ll End the Iran War in Weeks. What If He Can’t?
item.description: President Trump faces the possibility that at the end of his own two-to-three week window for wrapping up the war in Iran, nothing much will have changed.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Legal experts express concerns over ‘violations of international law’ in the war.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of an airstrike in Tehran, the Iranian capital, on Monday.

item.title: War With Iran Clarifies Trump’s Spending Priorities: The Military, Not Child Care
item.description: As the White House prepares to release its 2027 budget, President Trump said military protection, not social programs, took precedence.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A building in Tehran damaged by airstrikes. This week, President Trump said: “It’s not possible for us to take care of day care, Medicaid, Medicare, all of these individual things, they can do it on a state basis.”

item.title: Danish Warship Sunk by Britain’s Lord Nelson 225 Years Ago Is Found
item.description: The ship sank during the Battle of Copenhagen, an important moment in Danish and British history, and became the origin of a common saying.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: Morten Johansen, head of maritime archaeology at Denmark’s Viking Ship Museum, showing an insignia recovered from the wreckage of the Dannebroge, in Copenhagen on Tuesday.

item.title: Storm Dave Cometh. But Why Is It Called That?
item.description: The conventions for naming storms are complicated by a mishmash of rules, national quirks and language barriers.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: Storm Bram brought heavy rain and high winds in Carnlough, Northern Ireland, in December 2025. Storm Dave is headed to the United Kingdom this weekend.

item.title: Ancient Artifacts Stolen in Dutch Museum Heist Are Recovered
item.description: The golden helmet of Cotofenesti, a highly regarded artifact from Romania, and two elaborate golden bracelets were taken in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.media:credit: Sem Van Der Wal/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The recovered golden helmet of Cotofenesti and two gold bracelets on display in the Netherlands on Thursday.

item.title: Macron Snaps Back at Trump, Reflecting Europe’s Growing Anger Over Iran
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron of France is among several European officials to speak out about the accumulated effect of President Trump’s criticisms of NATO and his war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France and President Trump meeting during the United Nations General Assembly last year.

item.title: Iranian officials come out defiant after Trump’s war speech.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Leily Nikounazar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The funeral of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps navy commander in Tehran, on Wednesday.

item.title: Volunteers in Helicopter Rescue Hiker’s Dog After a Week in the Wilderness
item.description: A hiker in New Zealand fell 180 feet down a waterfall and was evacuated without her dog. A crowd-funded rescue effort reunited them.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:credit: Precision Helicopters Ltd, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A rescuer carrying Molly, the dog on the right, away from a remote waterfall on the Arahura River on the South Island of New Zealand, where she was found on Tuesday, a week after being separated from her owner.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler, Jason Karaian, Erika Solomon and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

item.title: 5 Takeaways From Trump’s Speech on Iran
item.description: President Trump did not define a clear path out of the conflict, which he estimated would end within three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump giving a prime-time address from the White House on Wednesday.

item.title: Transcript: Trump’s Speech on Iran War
item.description: The president made his case for the U.S. attack, and said that the main objectives had been achieved.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: U.S. Lifts Sanctions on Venezuela’s New Leader, Delcy Rodríguez
item.description: Removing sanctions would allow Delcy Rodríguez, the country’s acting leader, to conduct business with U.S. companies and potentially meet with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Trump administration removed Delcy Rodríguez, the acting president of Venezuela, from its sanctions list on Wednesday.

item.title: What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday.
item.description: Formidable strikes rocked Tehran early Wednesday morning. Iran launched what its state-aligned media called one of the largest attacks on Israel yet, damaging several cities.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of a deadly airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday.

item.title: In Speech, Trump Is Expected to Call Iran War a Success
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump in the Oval Office on Tuesday. He has given contradictory statements about his goals and endgame for the war.

item.title: Trump Initially Laid Out Five Goals for the Iran War. Here’s Where They Stand.
item.description: The United States and Israel have done significant damage to Iran’s military capabilities. But Iran still fires missiles, has nuclear material and coordinates with militias in the region.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new mural in Valiasr Square in Tehran on Wednesday. A large number of Iran’s ballistic missiles and launchers remain undamaged after a month of airstrikes by the United States and Israel.

item.title: Iran Is Skeptical About Diplomacy, U.S. Intelligence Says
item.description: Any decision by Iran to keep fighting would complicate President Trump’s stated goal of trying to end the war within weeks.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Multiple U.S. intelligence agencies have assessed that the Iranian government is not, for now, willing to engage in serious negotiations over ending the war.

item.title: Trump’s Endgame in Iran?
item.description: We look at what has changed, and what hasn’t, as a result of the joint U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Alicia P.Q. Wittmeyer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Stephen Lewis, Leftist Canadian Politician and AIDS Activist, Dies at 88
item.description: Part of a political dynasty, he led Ontario’s main progressive party, became Canada’s U.N. ambassador and campaigned against the spread of AIDS in Africa.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Tibor Kolley/Globe and Mail, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Stephen Lewis, left, in 2003, when he was a special United Nations envoy charged with overseeing anti-AIDS efforts in Africa.

item.title: Iran’s President Suggests Diplomatic Engagement Possible in Letter to U.S. Public
item.description: The letter, by President Masoud Pezeshkian of Iran, was at times defiant, patronizing or conciliatory, and came hours before President Trump was set to address the American people on the war.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Masoud Pezeshkian of Iran in Tehran in February.

item.title: Iraqi Kidnappers of Journalist Shelly Kittleson Offer to Negotiate
item.description: The Iranian-aligned militia, Kataib Hezbollah, is pressing for the release of members detained by the Iraqi government in exchange for freeing the journalist, Shelly Kittleson.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Falih Hassan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Shelly Kittleson in Baghdad on Tuesday.

item.title: ‘I Don’t Care About That’: Trump Says Iran’s Enriched Uranium Is Not a Concern
item.description: President Trump’s statement was the second time in 24 hours that he had declared that the nuclear problem with Iran had been solved, despite all evidence to the contrary.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: This morning, President Trump told Reuters that he didn’t care about Iran’s nuclear fuel because it is “so far underground.”

item.title: Heavy bombings across Tehran jolt its war-weary residents.
item.description: Some people in the capital said the blasts on Wednesday morning were among the most powerful they had felt in more than a month of war.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A view of Tehran on Wednesday after an airstrike.

item.title: Why Reopening the Strait of Hormuz Matters for the U.S. Economy
item.description: The interconnectedness of global energy markets means that the effects of Iran’s blockade of the waterway are not limited to countries directly dependent on oil from the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: Tankers anchored in Muscat, Oman, last month, as the Strait of Hormuz remained largely closed to shipping traffic.

item.title: Netanyahu makes the case that wars on Iran have succeeded.
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu claimed that Israel had crushed Iran’s capacity to make nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles. But Iran has continued attacking Israel and Persian Gulf countries and is believed to still have highly enriched uranium.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at a news conference in Jerusalem last month.

item.title: Without the U.S., what would happen to NATO?
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.media:description: American soldiers during a NATO exercise in Romania in June.

item.title: Trump Says Europe Is On Its Own in Securing the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Threatening to pull out of NATO, President Trump portrayed the alliance as a “paper tiger” and said Europe was on its own in trying to secure the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: President Trump has cast Europe as weak and toothless, and an ungrateful partner to the United States.

item.title: French Prosecutors Link Foiled Attack on American Bank to a Pro-Iranian Group
item.description: The group is suspected of involvement in a string of attacks on Jewish targets in Belgium, Britain and the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler and Lizzie Dearden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A police van outside a Bank of America office in Paris on Monday.

item.title: The Fall of a Cambodian Money-Laundering Giant
item.description: The former chairman of a financial group was brought to China in handcuffs, the latest high-level capture in a widening investigation into organized crime.
item.dc:creator: Selam Gebrekidan
item.category: Ministry of Public Security of the People's Republic of China
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Huione headquarters in Phnom Penh, Cambodia, in 2025. Huione Group and its affiliates have laundered at least $4 billion for criminals.

item.title: Robot Taxis Stop in Traffic in Chinese City, Stranding Travelers
item.description: The authorities in Wuhan, the site of one of the world’s largest experiments in self-driving cars, cited a “system failure” after widespread reports on Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Baidu Apollo robotaxi with painted flowers in Wuhan, China, in 2024. The cars have become a common sight on Wuhan roads.
